channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Tuchel confirms Rice fit for Argentina World Cup semi-final clash
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
England manager Thomas Tuchel confirms Declan Rice is fit to start the World Cup semi-final against Argentina after recovering from illness. Read more here
Read More: https://punchng.com/tuchel-confirms-rice-fit-for-argentina-world-cup-semi-final-clash/

item.title: I still try to call you, IK Ogbonna breaks silence on Ekubo’s death
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nollywood actor IK Ogbonna pays emotional tribute to his late friend Alex Ekubo, two months after his death, reflecting on loss and cherished memories.
Read More: https://punchng.com/i-still-try-to-call-you-ik-ogbonna-breaks-silence-on-ekubos-death/

item.title: England rugby players to avoid wearing national shirts in Argentina
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: England's rugby players have revealed they will avoid wearing national shirts in Argentina this week, ahead of facing the Pumas in Saturday's Nations Championship Test in Santiago del Estero.

item.description: Majeeed Lyrics Miss Me lyrics by Majeeed revolve around the emotional aftermath of a toxic relationship, highlighting themes of heartbreak, self-worth, and personal growth. The song expresses the pain of giving unconditional love and support to someone who failed to appreciate it, while conveying th

item.dc:creator: Daily Post

Some gunmen had abducted some school children and students of Baptist Nursery and Primary School, LA Primary School, and Community G

item.title: I have Kept My Promise To Ham People — Uba Sani
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Governor Uba Sani has broken a 20-year old jinx in Jaba local government, with the commissioning of five township roads in Kwoi, the headquarter of the Area Council.   The Governor recalled that when he performed the ground breaking ceremony last September, he assured that the intervention woul

item.title: Firefighters continue to battle 'contained' Fontainebleau fire near Paris
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Hundreds of French firefighters on Tuesday managed to contain two fires that have burned over 2,000 hectares of the historic Fontainebleau forest near Paris over the last two days. But the local prefect warned that the fires were not extinguished and firefighters will need to remain on the scene for

item.title: Lagos APM Backs Makinde’s Call For Independent Probe Into Oriire School Kidnapping
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Lagos State chapter of the Allied Peoples Movement (APM) has thrown its weight behind Oyo State Governor Seyi Makinde’s call for an independent investigation into the kidnapping of schoolchildren and teachers in Oriire Local Government Area. The party described opposition to the proposal b

item.title: Chinese growth in second quarter weakest since Covid-19 era
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Official data released on Wednesday shows China's GDP grew 4.3% in the second quarter, compared to a year prior. The last time quarterly growth was that weak was in late 2022, when China was feeling the impact of the Covid pandemic. Slow consumer demand, despite a surge in exports, helps explain the

item.title: More health workers strike as confirmed Ebola cases in DR Congo top 2,000
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Confirmed cases of Ebola in DR Congo have reached 2,011, including 754 deaths, according to government data in what authorities say is the fastest-growing outbreak on record. It comes as health workers at the Bunia General Hospital went on strike on Wednesday, the latest group to have walked off the

item.title: 'A tactical thrashing': Spain fans celebrate World Cup win over France
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Spain fans celebrated long into the night Tuesday after their side beat France 2-0 in the semi-final of the World Cup. After Mikel Oyarzabal's penalty in the 22nd minute, Pedro Porro doubled Spain's lead in the 58th, dumping out a team many had tipped to win the tournament.

item.description: The federal government has launched the National Guidelines for Public Procurement of Food and Related Services to promote healthier diets, improve nutrition and reduce the burden of diet-related diseases across the country. At the launch in Abuja, the Minister of State for Health and Social Welfare

item.title: Transfers: Super Eagles star eyes move to Ligue 1, Turkey & Portugal after salary cut dispute
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: The Super Eagles defender is one of only a few Nigerian stars to feature in the French top flight last term Nigeria international Chidozie Awaziem has informed FC Nantes that he wants to leave the French club this summer after refusing to accept the salary reduction that comes with their relegation

item.title: Netflix's next growth chapter hinges on keeping viewers hooked
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: US expands sanctions targeting Iran oil, cryptocurrency sectors
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The United States on Tuesday expanded its sanctions targeting Iran's oil sector, taking further aim at the network of petroleum shipping magnate Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani, the Treasury Department said. The post US expands sanctions targeting Iran oil, cryptocurrency sectors appeared first on Vangua

item.title: Why I Missed Out on Signing Drake — Akon
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: International music mogul Akon has opened up about his near-signing of Canadian rap icon Drake around 2008, revealing that he missed the opportunity because of a $1 million price tag.   Speaking in a recent interview with The Shade Room, the Senegalese-American singer explained that the barrier

item.title: The PS6 sure sounds like a handheld
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: The video game industry is in turmoil. Microsoft and Sony are starting to pivot to their next consoles, but it's not looking great: Prices are soaring, Sony is killing the video game disc, and Microsoft is jettisoning studios ahead of the transition. What could entice people to pay? On the Xbox fron

item.title: Belgian star Meunier signs for Premier League side Sunderland
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The 34-year-old -- one of Belgium's 'golden generation' whose last hurrah was a World Cup quarter-final exit to Spain -- signed a two-year contract. The post Belgian star Meunier signs for Premier League side Sunderland appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Kenyan court dismisses Rastafarians' bid to legalise cannabis
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The court acknowledged the need for a broader national debate on cannabis policy in Kenya.

item.title: Delta non-oil sector increasingly contributing to economic growth – Emu, SSG
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Kingsley Emu, Secretary to Delta State Government says the Government is deliberately repositioning its economy beyond its traditional identity as read more Delta non-oil sector increasingly contributing to economic growth – Emu, SSG

item.title: Rice Declared Fit To Start For England In World Cup Semi-Final
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The Arsenal midfielder has been struggling with illness and came off at half-time in the quarter-final victory over Norway. The post Rice Declared Fit To Start For England In World Cup Semi-Final appeared first on Channels Television.
